sources.expression: $wpdb->get_col( $wpdb->prepare( "SELECT ID FROM {$wpdb->posts} WHERE ( post_type IN ( $types ) ) AND post_status = 'publish' ORDER BY ID DESC LIMIT %d," . WPSC_PRELOAD_POST_COUNT, $c ) )
$wpdb->get_var( "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {$wpdb->posts} WHERE post_type IN ( $post_type_list ) AND post_status = 'publish'" )

sinks.expression: printf( esc_html__( 'The file %s was created by the Jetpack Boost plugin.', 'wp-super-cache' ), esc_html( $wpsc_advanced_cache_filename ) )
printf( esc_html__( 'Deactivate the "Cache Site Pages" module of Jetpack Boost on the %s page.', 'wp-super-cache' ), '' . esc_html__( 'Boost Settings', 'wp-super-cache' ) . '' )

printf( __( 'The free %1$sJetpack plugin%2$s has a %3$sSite Accelerator%2$s feature that is easier to use than the CDN functionality in this plugin. However files will be cached "forever" and will not update if you update the local file. Files will need to be renamed to refresh them. The %3$sJetpack documentation%2$s explains more about this.', 'wp-super-cache' ), '', '', '' )
printf( __( 'You already have Jetpack installed and %1$sSite Accelerator%2$s enabled on this blog. The CDN here is disabled to avoid conflicts with Jetpack.', 'wp-super-cache' ), '', '' )
printf( __( 'You already have Jetpack installed but %1$sSite Accelerator%2$s is disabled on this blog. Enable it on the %3$sJetpack settings page%2$s.', 'wp-super-cache' ), '', '', '' )

printf( __( '%1$sJetpack%2$s was not found on your site but %3$syou can install it%2$s. The Site Accelerator feature is free to use on any WordPress site and offers the same benefit as other CDN services. You should give it a try!', 'wp-super-cache' ), '', '', '' )
printf( __( 'WARNING: Test some static urls e.g., %s to ensure your CDN service is fully working before saving changes.', 'wp-super-cache' ), '' . esc_html( $example_cdn_uri ) . '' )

printf( __( 'WARNING: Your siteurl and homeurl are different. The plugin is using %s as the homepage URL of your site but if that is wrong please use the filter "ossdl_off_blog_url" to fix it.', 'wp-super-cache' ), '' . esc_html( $ossdl_off_blog_url ) . '' )

printf( __( 'The new URL to be used in place of %1$s for rewriting. No trailing / please.
Example: %2$s.', 'wp-super-cache' ), esc_html( get_site_url() ), esc_html( $example_cdn_uri ) )

printf( __( 'These CNAMES will be used in place of %1$s for rewriting (in addition to the off-site URL above). Use a comma as the delimiter. For pages with a large number of static files, this can improve browser performance. CNAMEs may also need to be configured on your CDN.
Example: %2$s', 'wp-super-cache' ), esc_html( get_site_url() ), esc_html( $example_cnames ) )
printf( __( 'DO NOT CACHE PAGE secret key: %s', 'wp-super-cache' ), trailingslashit( get_bloginfo( 'url' ) ) . "?donotcachepage={$cache_page_secret}", $cache_page_secret )
printf( __( 'Change the location of your cache files. The default is WP_CONTENT_DIR . /cache/ which translates to %s.', 'wp-super-cache' ), WP_CONTENT_DIR . '/cache/' )

printf( __( 'Since you are using mod_rewrite to serve cache files, you must choose a directory in your web root which is <q>%s</q> and update the mod_rewrite rules in the .htaccess file.', 'wp-super-cache' ), ABSPATH )

printf( __( 'If uninstalling this plugin, make sure the directory %s is writeable by the webserver so the files advanced-cache.php and cache-config.php can be deleted automatically. (Making sure those files are writeable is probably a good idea!)', 'wp-super-cache' ), esc_attr( WP_CONTENT_DIR ) )
printf( __( 'Please see the readme.txt for instructions on uninstalling this script. Look for the heading, "How to uninstall WP Super Cache".', 'wp-super-cache' ), plugins_url() )

printf( __( 'WP Super Cache mod rewrite rules were detected in your %s.htaccess file.
Click the following link to see the lines added to that file. If you have upgraded the plugin, make sure these rules match.', 'wp-super-cache' ), $home_path )
printf( __( 'UTC time is %s', 'wp-super-cache' ), date_i18n( $timezone_format, false, 'gmt' ) )
printf( esc_html__( 'See how your site performs by doing a %s analysis of it.', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'GTMetrix' )
printf( esc_html__( 'Advanced users only: Install an object cache like %s.', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'Memcached' )
printf( __( 'WP Crontrol is a useful plugin to use when trying to debug garbage collection and preload problems.', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-crontrol/' )
printf( __( 'You must make %s writable to enable this feature. As this is a security risk, please make it read-only after your page is generated.', 'wp-super-cache' ), ABSPATH )
printf( __( '%s is writable. Please make it readonly after your page is generated as this is a security risk.', 'wp-super-cache' ), ABSPATH )
header( 'X-WP-Super-Cache: Served supercache gzip file from PHP' )
header( 'X-WP-Super-Cache: Served supercache 304 file from PHP' )
header( 'X-WP-Super-Cache: Served supercache file from PHP' )

printf( __( 'You or an administrator may be able to make it work by changing the group owner of the plugin scripts to match that of the web server user. The group owner of the %s/cache/ directory must also be changed. See the safe mode manual page for further details.', 'wp-super-cache' ), esc_attr( WP_CONTENT_DIR ) )

printf( __( 'Your server thinks your hostname resolves to %s. Some services such as garbage collection by this plugin, and WordPress scheduled posts may not operate correctly.', 'wp-super-cache' ), $ip )
printf( __( 'Please see entry 16 in the Troubleshooting section of the readme.txt', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-super-cache/faq/' )
printf( __( 'Please see entry 16 in the Troubleshooting section of the readme.txt', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'https://wordpress.org/plugins/wp-super-cache/faq/' )

printf( __( 'The WP Super Cache configuration file is %s/wp-cache-config.php and cannot be modified. That file must be writeable by the web server to make any changes.', 'wp-super-cache' ), WPCACHECONFIGPATH )
printf( __( 'You should change the permissions on %s and make it more restrictive. Use your ftp client, or the following command to fix things:', 'wp-super-cache' ), WP_CONTENT_DIR )

printf( wp_kses( __( 'Delete the plugin mod_rewrite rules in %s.htaccess enclosed by # BEGIN WPSuperCache and # END WPSuperCache and let the plugin regenerate them by reloading this page.', 'wp-super-cache' ), array( 'code' => array() ) ), esc_html( $home_path ) )

printf( wp_kses( __( 'Add the rules yourself. Edit %1$s.htaccess and find the block of code enclosed by the lines # BEGIN WPSuperCache and # END WPSuperCache. There are two sections that look very similar. Just below the line %%{HTTP:Cookie} !^.*(comment_author_|%2$s|wp-postpass_).*$ add these lines: (do it twice, once for each section)', 'wp-super-cache' ), array( 'code' => array() ) ), esc_html( $home_path ), esc_html( wpsc_get_logged_in_cookie() ) )
printf( __( 'Use the Debug tab for diagnostics.', 'wp-super-cache' ), admin_url( 'options-general.php?page=wpsupercache&tab=debug' ) )
printf( __( 'Try out the development version for the latest fixes (changelog).', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'https://odd.blog/y/2o', 'https://plugins.trac.wordpress.org/log/wp-super-cache/' )
printf( __( 'Please rate us and give feedback.', 'wp-super-cache' ), 'https://wordpress.org/support/plugin/wp-super-cache/reviews?rate=5#new-post' )
printf( __( 'Cached pages since %1$s : %2$s', 'wp-super-cache' ), date( 'M j, Y', $start_date ), number_format( get_option( 'wpsupercache_count' ) ) )

printf( '
  • %s
    • ', esc_attr( $tab === $current ? 'wpsc-nav-tab wpsc-nav-tab-selected' : 'wpsc-nav-tab' ), esc_url_raw( add_query_arg( 'tab', $tab, $admin_url ) ), esc_html( $name ) )
wp_die( __( 'Warning! You are not allowed to delete that file', 'wp-super-cache' ) )
$wpdb->get_col( $wpdb->prepare( "SELECT ID FROM {$wpdb->posts} WHERE ( post_type IN ( $types ) ) AND post_status = 'publish' ORDER BY ID DESC LIMIT %d," . WPSC_PRELOAD_POST_COUNT, $c ) )
wp_die( "Could not remove WP_CACHE define from $global_config_file. Please edit that file and remove the line containing the text 'WP_CACHE'. Then refresh this page." )
wp_die( "Could not remove WPCACHEHOME define from $global_config_file. Please edit that file and remove the line containing the text 'WPCACHEHOME'. Then refresh this page." )
$wpdb->get_var( "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {$wpdb->posts} WHERE post_type IN ( $post_type_list ) AND post_status = 'publish'" )

sanitizers.expression: esc_html( sprintf( __( 'Many emails, 2 emails per %d posts.', 'wp-super-cache' ), WPSC_PRELOAD_POST_COUNT ) )
esc_html( sprintf( __( 'Medium, 1 email per %d posts.', 'wp-super-cache' ), WPSC_PRELOAD_POST_COUNT ) )

wp_kses( __( 'Delete the plugin mod_rewrite rules in %s.htaccess enclosed by # BEGIN WPSuperCache and # END WPSuperCache and let the plugin regenerate them by reloading this page.', 'wp-super-cache' ), array( 'code' => array() ) )

wp_kses( __( 'Add the rules yourself. Edit %1$s.htaccess and find the block of code enclosed by the lines # BEGIN WPSuperCache and # END WPSuperCache. There are two sections that look very similar. Just below the line %%{HTTP:Cookie} !^.*(comment_author_|%2$s|wp-postpass_).*$ add these lines: (do it twice, once for each section)', 'wp-super-cache' ), array( 'code' => array() ) )
$wpdb->prepare( "SELECT ID FROM {$wpdb->posts} WHERE ( post_type IN ( $types ) ) AND post_status = 'publish' ORDER BY ID DESC LIMIT %d," . WPSC_PRELOAD_POST_COUNT, $c )
